feature: Inventory and dependency graph: service auto-discovery / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on service auto-discovery cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for service auto-discovery: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover service auto-discovery, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for service auto-discovery.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for service auto-discovery.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Applications and Microservices APM service auto-discovery inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: service-auto-discovery_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for service auto-discovery cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for service auto-discovery; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for service auto-discovery.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for service auto-discovery, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for service auto-discovery.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for service auto-discovery.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing service auto-discovery data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling service auto-discovery inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for service auto-discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Given service auto-discovery data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: service auto-discovery connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for error tracking: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for error tracking security posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for error tracking.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for error tracking.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Applications and Microservices APM error tracking automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: error-tracking_security_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for error tracking security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for error tracking; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for error tracking.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for error tracking recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for error tracking.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for error tracking.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing error tracking data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling error tracking automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for error tracking.
acceptance_criteria: Given error tracking data exists, when the security automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: error tracking connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for hot path detection: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for hot path detection with resilience guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for hot path detection.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for hot path detection.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Applications and Microservices APM hot path detection operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: hot-path-detection_resilience_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for hot path detection resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for hot path detection; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for hot path detection.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for hot path detection recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for hot path detection.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for hot path detection.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing hot path detection data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling hot path detection operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for hot path detection.
acceptance_criteria: Given hot path detection data exists, when the resilience operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: hot path detection connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for JVM runtime metrics: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect JVM runtime metrics symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect resilience.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for JVM runtime metrics.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for JVM runtime metrics.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Applications and Microservices APM JVM runtime metrics health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: jvm-runtime-metrics_resilience_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for JVM runtime metrics resilience that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for JVM runtime metrics; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for JVM runtime metrics.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for JVM runtime metrics recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for JVM runtime metrics.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for JVM runtime metrics.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing JVM runtime metrics data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling JVM runtime metrics health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for JVM runtime metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Given JVM runtime metrics data exists, when the resilience health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: JVM runtime metrics connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for feature flag correlation: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Create repeatable runbooks and scheduled checks for feature flag correlation scalability posture.
backend_scope: Add runbook schema, scheduler hooks, history, and notification events for feature flag correlation.
frontend_scope: Add runbook editor, schedule controls, history table, and owner assignment for feature flag correlation.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Applications and Microservices APM feature flag correlation automation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: feature-flag-correlation_scalability_automation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for feature flag correlation scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for feature flag correlation; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for feature flag correlation.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for feature flag correlation recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for feature flag correlation.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for feature flag correlation.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing feature flag correlation data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling feature flag correlation automation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for feature flag correlation.
acceptance_criteria: Given feature flag correlation data exists, when the scalability automation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: feature flag correlation connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for service health score: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Detect service health score symptoms, thresholds, anomalies, and correlated events that affect cost.
backend_scope: Add deterministic evaluators, metrics ingestion, and explainable findings for service health score.
frontend_scope: Add health badges, charts, timelines, and affected-object drilldowns for service health score.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Applications and Microservices APM service health score health with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: service-health-score_cost_health evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for service health score cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for service health score; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for service health score.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for service health score, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for service health score.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for service health score.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing service health score data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling service health score health broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for service health score.
acceptance_criteria: Given service health score data exists, when the cost health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: service health score connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for service workbench; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for service workbench.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for service workbench recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for service workbench.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for service workbench.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing service workbench data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling service workbench tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for service workbench.
acceptance_criteria: Given service workbench data exists, when the operational-excellence tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: service workbench connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
